title: 10 best herbal teas for hypothyroidism

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chinese Thoroughwort Tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to improved thyroid function.

Adaptogenic properties: The tea's adaptogenic compounds, such as glycosides and flavonoids, may help regulate the body's response to stress, which is often linked to hypothyroidism, and promote a sense of balance and well-being.

Antioxidant properties: Chinese Thoroughwort Tea's antioxidant compounds, including vitamin C and polyphenols, may help neutralize free radicals that can damage thyroid tissue and disrupt thyroid function, thereby supporting overall thyroid health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bupleurum falcatum, also known as Chinese thoroughwort, teas helps with hypothyroidism because it is believed to stimulate the thyroid gland and improve its function. The herbal tea contains compounds that may help to regulate thyroid hormone production and increase metabolism. Additionally, it is thought to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, a common issue in people with hypothyroidism. Regular consumption of Bupleurum falcatum tea may help to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism, such as fatigue and weight gain.
remedy_constituents: Chrysophanol: This is a stilbenoid compound that has been shown to stimulate the activity of the thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH), which is often decreased in individuals with hypothyroidism.

Saikosaponins: These are triterpenoid saponins that have been found to have a regulatory effect on thyroid function by increasing the expression of genes involved in thyroid hormone production and metabolism.

Chlorogenic acid: This is a phenolic compound that has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative damage and promote the production of thyroid hormones.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are primarily used due to their rich content of saikosaponins, which are believed to stimulate the thyroid gland and improve its function.
Leaves: They are used for their bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, which may help regulate thyroid hormone production and improve overall thyroid health.
Barks: They are employed due to their content of lignans and other polyphenolic compounds, which may help reduce inflammation and promote thyroid function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried bupleurum falcatum root and clean it thoroughly with a soft brush.
Chop the cleaned bupleurum falcatum root into small pieces and mix with 500 milliliters of boiling water.
Steep the chopped bupleurum falcatum root in boiling water for 15-20 minutes to allow for extraction.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh to remove the solids from the liquid.
Store the herbal tea in the refrigerator to consume within 24 hours and serve chilled.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Herbs like Ashwagandha, Ginkgo Biloba, and Rhodiola Rosea contain adaptogenic compounds that help regulate stress hormones, which can alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue, anxiety, and depression.

Thyroid-stimulating properties: Certain herbs like Bladderwrack, Kelp, and Dulse contain iodine, which is essential for thyroid function, and may help stimulate the production of thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3).

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Herbs like Chamomile, Lavender, and Lemon Balm have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which are common complications of hypothyroidism.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calea sp c, also known as dream herb, teas helps with hypothyroidism because it supports the thyroid gland's function by promoting the production of thyroid hormones. The tea is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hypothyroidism. Additionally, Calea sp c tea has adaptogenic properties, helping the body adapt to stress and promoting a balanced endocrine system, ultimately supporting thyroid health and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Calein: A sesquiterpene lactone found in Calea z, calein has been shown to stimulate the thyroid gland, thereby promoting the production of thyroid hormones and potentially alleviating symptoms of hypothyroidism.

Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound, quercetin is known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypothyroidism, thus supporting thyroid function.

Ligustilide: A sesquiterpene lactone with sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, ligustilide has been reported to stimulate the secretion of thyroid hormones and improve thyroid function in animal studies, suggesting its potential benefits in managing hypothyroidism.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, which may help support thyroid function.
Flowers: Utilized for their ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can be beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism.
Roots: Employed for their purported ability to stimulate the thyroid gland and support thyroid hormone production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the calea sp c flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when the temperature is cooler.
Dry the calea sp c flowers in a single layer on paper bags or a clean cloth for 1 to 2 weeks.
Measure out 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried calea sp c flowers per 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the dried calea sp c flowers in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then serve the calea sp c tea warm or chilled.

remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, contributing to improved thyroid function.

remedy_recipe: Choose high-quality dried ginkgo biloba leaves with a recommended dosage of 1 teaspoon per 8 ounces of water.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the leaves and discard them to avoid any sediment.
Add honey or sugar to taste if desired to sweeten the tea without altering its properties.
Serve the tea immediately and consume it within 30 minutes to 1 hour for optimal effects.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh scrophularia nodosa leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly with water.
Boil one teaspoon of the dried herb in 8 ounces of water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine mesh to remove the herb particles and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to release the active compounds and flavors.
Consume the tea immediately after preparation to enjoy its potential health benefits.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried clematis vitalba stems and leaves in late summer or early fall when they are most potent.

Crush 2 to 3 teaspoons of dried clematis vitalba into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in a cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it immediately while still warm for maximum benefits.

Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids immediately.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Drink two cups of cooled tea per day for at least six weeks to alleviate hypothyroid symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with hypothyroidism because it contains a blend of herbs that naturally stimulate the thyroid gland, promoting its function and production of hormones. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality tea leaves of camellia sinensis, ensuring optimal l-theanine content for relaxation.
Measure two teaspoons of dried green tea leaves and one teaspoon of dried white tea leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Heat filtered water to 180-190 degrees fahrenheit and steep the tea mixture for six to eight minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the loose leaves, and add one tablespoon of ashwagandha powder for enhanced thyroid support.
Allow the tea to cool to 100-110 degrees fahrenheit before consumption, as excessive heat can degrade l-theanine's benefits for hypothyroidism sufferers.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality artemisia absinthium leaves from a reputable supplier or grow your own plants.
Rinse the leaves gently with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris and pat dry with a paper towel.
Combine 15-20 grams of dried artemisia absinthium leaves with 500 ml boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hydrastis canadensis roots for the tea preparation according to botanical guidelines.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator at 60°f for 24 hours.
Grind 1 teaspoon of dried hydrastis canadensis root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Steep the ground hydrastis canadensis powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a decoction.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30-40 minutes of steeping time.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hypothyroidism that are treated with herbal teas are adrenal fatigue, adrenal insufficiency, and chronic fatigue syndrome. Herbal teas such as Ashwagandha, Holy Basil, and Ginseng can help alleviate symptoms like fatigue, weakness, and mental fogginess. These herbs have adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress and promote energy production. Additionally, teas like Licorice Root and Dandelion Root are used to support adrenal function and balance hormone production.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hypothyroidism are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of this endocrine disorder by stimulating the thyroid gland and promoting hormonal balance. The best herbal teas for hypothyroidism include Astragalus membranaceus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Schisandra chinensis, Withania somnifera, Cassia auriculata, Panax ginseng, Silybum marianum, Cassia angustifolia, Ligusticum wallichii, and Foeniculum vulgare. If left untreated, hypothyroidism can lead to fatigue, weight gain, hair loss, and depression, negatively impacting daily life, relationships, and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with hypothyroidism is a blend of ashwagandha, licorice root, and bladderwrack. Ashwagandha helps balance thyroid hormones, while licorice root supports adrenal function and reduces inflammation. Bladderwrack, a type of seaweed, is rich in iodine, essential for thyroid function. Drinking these teas together may help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism, such as fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance. Consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies to ensure safe and effective treatment.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Current drug discovery technologies, the herbal medicine has shown potential in regulating thyroid hormones and preventing side effects associated with primary hypothyroidism. The study involved a systematic review of 4 randomized clinical trials with 186 participants, which included the use of Nigella sativa L., ashwagandha, and Mentha x Piperita L. The results found that Nigella sativa L. caused a significant decrease in weight and body mass index (BMI), and increased T3 levels, while ashwagandha significantly increased T3 and T4 levels, and decreased TSH levels.
